page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, translating to a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on external storage solutions and network-based resources. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average multi-core performance but exhibits weaker single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test and single-threaded gzip compression benchmarks place the processor in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Conversely, multi-core operations, such as the stress-ng multi-core test and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, achieve average tier results. Memory performance is stable, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 1557.61 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 47.96 GB/sec. Database and web serving operations also perform within the average range, with Redis SET operations handling 26.78 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 20.38 GB/sec.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.c6.8xlarge is structured for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from parallel processing rather than high memory capacity per core. The lack of bundled local storage and GPU accelerators makes it less suitable for heavy database storage or deep learning tasks. Instead, it is best aligned with multi-threaded applications, web serving, and distributed computing tasks that can leverage its 32 dedicated cores.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network performance is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression are weak, placing in the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform consistently in the average tier. With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized, compute-bound workloads rather than memory-intensive applications. It is well-suited for multi-threaded processing, static web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for multi-threaded and parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.8xlarge features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 64 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core compute-optimized instance with a balanced memory-to-core ratio for parallelized processing and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and parallel computing
